title: The Carbon Budget Game
description: Earth has a limited carbon budget to stay below 1.5°C. Spend wisely — or watch it run out.

stages.title: You Have a Budget. It's Running Out.
stages.body:
Imagine Earth's atmosphere like a bathtub. The drain (natural carbon sinks — oceans, forests) removes ~5.9 GtC/year. But we're pouring in 10.4 GtC/year from fossil fuels and land use.

The carbon budget is how much more CO₂ we can emit while keeping warming below 1.5°C. According to the IPCC AR6:

🔴 Remaining budget from 2023: ~200 GtCO₂

At current rates, we'll exhaust this in roughly 5 years. After that, we are almost certainly locked into warming above 1.5°C.
stages.gaia: We're spending a 500-year budget in 200 years. And we're accelerating. The bathtub is overflowing — and we're still turning up the tap.

stages.title: Allocate the Carbon Budget
stages.description: You control how the remaining 200 GtCO₂ budget is spent across sectors. Every choice has consequences. Adjust each slider — but remember, the total can't exceed 200 GtCO₂ if we want to stay under 1.5°C.
stages.gaia: Here's the uncomfortable truth: even if you set every slider to zero except the minimum, you'd still struggle to stay within budget. That's not a design flaw — that's the math.

stages.title: How Does Your Budget Look?
stages.description: The gauge shows your total allocation against the 200 GtCO₂ limit.
stages.gaugeContext: The green zone means we're within budget. Yellow means we're cutting it close. Red means we've blown past 1.5°C. Most allocations end up in the red — because the budget is that tight.

stages.title: Budget Check

questions.question: What is the remaining carbon budget for 1.5°C (from 2023)?
questions.explanation: The IPCC AR6 assessed ~200 GtCO₂ remaining for a 50% chance of limiting warming to 1.5°C.

questions.question: At 2023 emission rates, how many years until the 1.5°C budget is exhausted?
Which sector contributes the most to annual emissions?
questions.explanation: Fossil fuel combustion accounts for ~37 of the ~40 GtCO₂/year total.

stages.completionText: You now understand the brutal math: we're spending carbon 3-4x faster than we should be. The remaining budget is tiny, and every fraction of a degree matters.

stages.title: Budget Understood. Now Let's Fix It.
stages.body:
You now understand the brutal math: we're spending carbon 3-4x faster than we should be. The remaining budget is tiny, and every fraction of a degree matters.

But here's the hope — nature-based solutions and restoration projects are already removing carbon from the atmosphere at scale. Let's see what's happening on the ground.
